row1.revenue: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) leads the pack with $716.9B in revenue, narrowly surpassing Walmart Inc. (WMT) by $3.7B. The top two companies hold over 40% of the combined revenue of the top five, with a significant gap to the third-ranked UnitedHealth Group Incorporated (UNH) at $447.6B.
row1.net_income: Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) leads the pack with a net income of $132.2B USD, a significant gap from NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) at $120.1B USD. This 9.6% difference highlights the substantial revenue advantage of Alphabet's diversified business model.
row1.total_assets: JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM) leads the pack with $4.4T in Total Assets, a significant gap of over $1T from Bank of America Corporation (BAC) in second place. This dominance underscores JPM's substantial market presence and financial muscle.
row1.total_equity: Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) holds the top spot with Total Equity of $415.3B USD, maintaining a significant lead over Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) with a $3.8B USD gap.
row1.total_liabilities: JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM) leads with $4.1T in Total Liabilities, a significant gap from Bank of America Corporation (BAC) at $3.1T, indicating a substantial difference in financial leverage among the top companies.
row1.cash_and_equivalents: JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM) leads with $343.3B in Cash And Equivalents, a significant gap from the second-ranked Bank of America Corporation (BAC) with $239.3B. JPM's cash reserves are nearly 43% higher than BAC's.
row1.gross_profit: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) leads the pack with a Gross Profit of 360.5B USD, a significant 49.4% gap above second-place Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) at 240.3B USD. This substantial margin highlights AMZN's dominance in the Gross Profit space.
row1.operating_income_ebit: Apple Inc. (AAPL) leads the top companies with an Operating Income Ebit of 133.1B USD, a significant margin above NVIDIA Corporation's (NVDA) 130.4B USD. The leader maintains a notable 2.7B USD gap over the second-place company.

row2_sector.revenue: Consumer Cyclical and Technology sectors dominate revenue, accounting for 43.1% of the total, with a combined market share of 43.1%. Consumer Cyclical's 22.0% share is the largest individual sector contribution.
row2_sector.total_assets: Financial Services sector dominates the total assets landscape with a 64.1% share, dwarfing the next closest sector by a significant margin. The sector's substantial size may indicate a high level of financial interconnectedness and concentration within the industry.
row2_sector.net_income: Technology sector dominates net income generation with 39.2% share, nearly double the second-largest sector, Communication Services, at 24.8%. The top two sectors (Technology and Communication Services) account for 64% of total net income.

row2_bubble: NVDA's 55.6% net margin stands out among peers, driven by its 215.9B USD revenue, while UNH's 2.7% net margin indicates lower profitability efficiency.
row3_heatmap: Morgan Stanley (MA) stands out for its exceptional return on equity (193.5%), while JPMorgan Chase (JPM) exhibits high leverage risk with a debt-to-equity ratio of 11.21x.
row4_market_trends: Alphabet's 12-month return of +102.0% stands out as the highest among the largest revenue companies, outperforming the market by a significant margin. Conversely, Adobe's -41.9% return is the lowest, indicating a substantial decline in its stock value over the past year. This stark contrast highlights the varying fortunes of these high-revenue companies.
